mismatched_fact_examples.truth_value: Dislocation density in Cr-containing as-cast strips is higher due to reduced κ-carbides which hinder dislocation formation under thermal stress, combined with increased solid solubility of C from Cr addition. In 9Cr, Cr23C6 precipitation reduces dislocation formation.
mismatched_fact_examples.prediction_value: Slip band statistics after tensile fracture: as-cast 0Cr average spacing ~90 nm and width 13.4 nm; as-cast 6Cr average spacing ~80 nm and width 12.5 nm; annealed 0Cr average spacing ~190 nm and width 20.5 nm; annealed 6Cr average spacing ~60 nm and width 7.9 nm. Finer slip bands and smaller spacing correspond to higher ductility (dynamic slip band refinement, DSBR), but high initial dislocation density in as-cast 6Cr interferes with this relationship.

mismatched_fact_examples.truth_value: For 3Cr steel, annealing at 550 °C for 1 h mainly precipitates κ and increases YS; longer time (9 h) reduces dislocation density and improves plasticity but risks coarsening κ.
